source_title: PushLock Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair
text: PushLock ® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair Surgical Technique 02 | PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair PushLock® Knotless Suture Anchors Uniquely designed for a secure knotless labral repair, the PushLock anchor eliminates the risk of knot impingement after arthroscopic labral repair. With this technique, independently pass suture through the capsule or labrum and adjust tissue tension prior to anchor insertion. Advantages ͸ Knotless techniques minimize the number of surgical steps and save time ͸ Designed specifically for glenoid labral repair to maximize the preservation of glenoid bone ͸ No risk of knot impingement ͸ Cannulated design minimizes anchor volume ͸ Suture tension is visualized and adjusted prior to anchor insertion ͸ Available in biocomposite or PEEK material and multiple sizes (2.4 mm and 2.9 mm short) BioComposite Short 2.9 mm PushLock Anchor With LabralTape™ Suture Knotless Simple Stitch Knotless Mattress Stitch Knotless Cinch Stitch Various premium suture options—FiberWire ®, SutureTape, or LabralTape—provide flexibility when choosing stitch configurations for soft-tissue repair (shown below). PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair | 03 01 Insert the SutureLasso™ suture passer into a cannula and pass it through the capsulolabral tissue. Advance the nitinol wire loop through the suture passer and retrieve it through a separate portal. 01 Insert the SutureLasso suture passer, which is preloaded with FiberStick suture, into a cannula and pass it through the capsulolabral tissue. Advance the suture through the suture passer and retrieve it through a separate portal using a KingFisher ® grasper. 02 Load the suture through the nitinol wire loop. Retract the suture passer and wire, shuttling the suture through the tissue. Retrieve both suture tails through the anchor insertion cannula. 02 Retrieve both suture tails through the anchor insertion cannula. Simple Stitch Repair With FiberWire®, SutureTape, or LabralTape™ Suture Simple Stitch Repair With FiberStick™ Suture 04 | PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair 01 Insert the SutureLasso™ suture passer into a cannula and pass it through the capsulolabral tissue. Advance the nitinol wire loop through the suture passer and retrieve it through a separate portal. 03 Pass the tail through the loop to create the cinch stitch. Pull on the tail to tighten and position the cinch stitch on the labrum. 02 Load the suture loop through the nitinol wire loop. Retract the suture passer and wire, shuttling the suture through the tissue. Retrieve both suture tails through the anchor insertion cannula. FiberLink SutureTape ͸ 1.3 mm and 1.7 mm sizes available ͸ Features a flat-tape loop that transitions to a round suture tail Cinch Stitch (Luggage Tag) Repair With FiberLink™ Suture or FiberLink SutureTape PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair | 05 01 Insert the SutureLasso™ suture passer, which is preloaded with SutureTape FiberStick suture, into a cannula and pass it through the capsulolabral tissue. Advance the suture through the suture passer and retrieve it through a separate portal. 03 Retrieve both suture tails through the anchor insertion cannula. Prior to preparing the bone, pass the suture tails through the PushLock® eyelet suture passer. Shuttle the suture tails and secure the suture with a hemostat. 02 Retract the suture passer, without removing it from the joint, and pass it again through the tissue to achieve the desired mattress spacing and orientation. Retrieve the suture from the suture passer using a FiberTape ® KingFisher® grasper. Mattress Stitch Repair With SutureTape FiberStick™ Suture 06 | PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair 01 Insert the spear through the cannula with the passed suture, placing it onto the glenoid rim. Fully advance the drill through the spear until its collar makes contact with the spear’s handle. Advance the PushLock anchor into the joint and tension the suture to approximate the labral tissue to the eyelet. 03 Remove the orange packaging clip. Tap the metal button on the driver handle to advance the anchor body until the proximal laser line is flush with the bone. Remove the driver by rotating it counterclockwise for 6 full revolutions. 02 While releasing the suture tails, advance the driver into the bone socket until the anchor body contacts the bone. If additional tension is needed to reduce the labral tissue to the bone, pull on the suture tails while keeping a firm grasp of the driver. The final tension is attained when the anchor is in contact with the bone. 04 Cut the sutures flush using an open-ended FiberWire ® suture cutter. PushLock® Anchor Insertion Pass the suture tails through the threader Shuttle the suture tails Secure the suture with a hemostat PushLock® Suture Anchor for Knotless Instability Repair | 07 Ordering Information Suture Anchors BioComposite PushLock® Anchor, 2.4 mm × 11.3 mm AR-2922BC PEEK PushLock Anchor, 2.4 mm × 11.3 mm AR-2922PS BioComposite Short PushLock Anchor, 2.9 mm × 12.5 mm AR-2923BC PEEK Short PushLock Anchor, 2.9
